title: Sandwich, Signed and Sealed: Mullin Survives the Sextuple
title_candidates: Sandwich, Signed and Sealed: Mullin Survives the Sextuple
Ten at the 16th: Alex Baker's Trophy Vanishes in the Dunes
theme: David Mullin wins TEG 14 not by sealing it himself but by being the one still standing after Alex Baker's late charge imploded on the 16th.
opening_hook: Open on the 16th green at Prince's, where Alex Baker — three behind, charging, the Trophy genuinely in reach — wrote down a 10. Justify the theme-led structure because this single hole is the round's, and the tournament's, decisive moment; everything else either led to it or reacted to it.
foreshadow: Mullin's quadruple bogey 8 at the 10th — the leader briefly looked breakable, and the Trophy gap began to shrink from eight
Alex Baker's blistering 10-point opening at 1-3 and another 11-point burst through 13-15 — the charge that made the 16th matter
Jon Baker's tear through 12-15 (+14 points) that briefly threatened to drag him out of Wooden Spoon range, before the 16th and 18th had their say

competition_state.name: Trophy
competition_state.note: Mullin wins the TEG 14 Trophy by 2 from Alex Baker. He started the day eight clear; a quad-bogey 8 at the 10th and a roaring Baker charge through the front nine and again at 13-15 cut the lead to the bone, but Baker's 10 at the 16th ended the contest in a single hole. Mullin won it by surviving it.

competition_state.name: Green Jacket
competition_state.note: Mullin completes the double, taking the Green Jacket by 7 over Jon Baker. The prior gap of 12 over Gregg Williams narrowed and the runner-up slot changed hands — Jon Baker's gross scoring (despite the 8 at the last) was strong enough to leapfrog Williams into second on the Jacket — but Mullin's lead was never seriously threatened on the gross card.

competition_state.name: Wooden Spoon
competition_state.note: Jon Baker takes the Wooden Spoon by 12 from Gregg Williams. He arrived 13 adrift and briefly threatened to make a race of it with +14 points across 12-15, but a double at the 16th and a quadruple-bogey 8 at the 18th ensured the cutlery drawer claimed him.

players.arc: The champion who wobbled at exactly the wrong time — a quadruple-bogey 8 at the 10th opened a door he never quite slammed shut — but held on to claim both Trophy and Green Jacket, the latter by a comfortable seven.

The nearly-man of TEG 14: 10 points in the first three holes, another 11 across 13-15, and the Trophy genuinely live — until a sextuple-bogey 10 at the 16th detonated everything and handed Mullin a two-shot win.

A round of two halves: a glorious +14 across 12-15 (two birdies, two pars) suggested an escape from the Spoon, before a double at 16 and a quad at the last confirmed his fate — though strong enough overall to climb to second on the Green Jacket.
Quietly overtaken on all fronts — slips from second to third on both Trophy and Green Jacket, and finishes just 12 clear of the Wooden Spoon he had looked safe from.

venue_notes: Third TEG visit to Prince's Shore/Dunes, the strongest routing at Sandwich — a proper links finish to a proper TEG, with the closing stretch (10, 16, 18 all featuring in the carnage) doing exactly what closing links holes are supposed to do. TEG's second visit to Kent.
